attractions.name: Atala Masjid
attractions.description: A 14th-century mosque known for its massive pylon-style architecture and intricate carvings.

attractions.name: Jaunpur Fort
attractions.description: An ancient fort built by Feroz Shah Tughlaq, offering panoramic views of the Gomti River.

attractions.name: Shahi Bridge
attractions.description: A historic 16th-century stone bridge built over the Gomti River by Munim Khan.

attractions.name: Jama Masjid
attractions.description: Also known as Bari Masjid, this is one of the largest and most impressive mosques in the region.
attractions.address: Jama Masjid Rd, Jaunpur, Uttar Pradesh

attractions.name: Lal Darwaza Masjid
attractions.description: A smaller but architecturally significant mosque built by the wife of Sultan Mahmud Sharqi.

attractions.name: Khalis Mukhlis Masjid
attractions.description: A mosque dating back to the Tughlaq era, known for its simple yet elegant design.

attractions.name: Sheetla Chaukiya Dham
attractions.description: A prominent Hindu temple dedicated to Goddess Sheetla, attracting many pilgrims.

attractions.name: Trilochan Mahadev Temple
attractions.description: An ancient Shiva temple located on the outskirts, known for its spiritual significance.

restaurants.name: Hotel River View Restaurant
restaurants.description: Offers a mix of North Indian and local delicacies with a view of the river.

restaurants.name: Shahi Restaurant
restaurants.description: Popular for traditional Mughlai dishes and kebabs.

restaurants.name: Bikaner Sweets & Restaurant
restaurants.description: Great for quick snacks, chaat, and traditional Indian sweets.

restaurants.name: New Zaika Restaurant
restaurants.description: A local favorite for biryani and non-vegetarian curries.

hidden_gems.name: Jhanjhari Masjid
hidden_gems.description: The ruins of a mosque that showcase the unique Sharqi architectural style in a quiet setting.

nightlife.name: Jaunpur City Center
nightlife.description: The area around the main market stays lively in the evening with street food vendors and local tea stalls.

shopping.name: Jaunpur Main Market
shopping.description: Ideal for purchasing local textiles, traditional handicrafts, and spices.

festivals.name: Imambara Processions
festivals.description: Significant religious processions held during Muharram.

adventure_activities.name: Gomti Riverbank Walk
adventure_activities.description: A peaceful walk along the riverbanks near the Shahi Bridge.

local_tips: Try the local 'Imarti' sweet, which is a specialty of Jaunpur.
Hire an auto-rickshaw for the day to visit the scattered historical monuments.
Dress modestly when visiting mosques and temples.

safety.general: Jaunpur is generally safe, but avoid isolated areas after dark.
safety.water_safety: Drink only bottled or purified water.
safety.transport_safety: Use registered auto-rickshaws and agree on the fare beforehand.

culture.overview: Jaunpur has a rich history as a center of Islamic learning and culture under the Sharqi dynasty.
culture.etiquette: Always remove footwear before entering religious sites and cover your head in mosques.

transport.getting_around: Auto-rickshaws and cycle-rickshaws are the primary modes of transport within the city.
transport.airport: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport in Varanasi (approx. 40km away).

visa_information.domestic_indian_travellers: No visa required; valid government ID is recommended for hotel check-ins.
visa_information.foreign_nationals: Valid Indian visa and passport required.

weather.best_time_to_visit: October to March, when the weather is cool and pleasant.
weather.avoid: April to June, as temperatures can become extremely hot.
